signals.headline: Raptors-Cavaliers Playoff Series Begins | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Raptors-Cavaliers Playoff Series Begins | YES | HIGH confidence**

The 2026 NBA Playoffs have officially begun with Game 1 of the first-round series between the Toronto Raptors (5 seed, 42-32) and Cleveland Cavaliers (4 seed, 52-30) tipping off today at 1:00 PM ET at Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse[1][2][3]. This is a best-of-seven series with Games 2 scheduled for Monday, April 20 at 7:00 PM[3].
matched_markets: NBA Playoffs: Raptors vs. Cavaliers Total Games O/U 5.5

signals.headline: Raptors Ruled Out Immanuel Quickley for Game 1 | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Raptors Ruled Out Immanuel Quickley for Game 1 | YES | HIGH confidence**

Quickley will miss the playoff opener due to a hamstring injury sustained during the regular-season finale against the Nets, with Jamal Shead starting in his place[2]. Quickley's next opportunity to play is Game 2 on Monday[2].

signals.headline: Cavaliers Heavy Favorites | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Cavaliers Heavy Favorites | YES | HIGH confidence**

Cleveland is favored by 8.5 points with an over/under of 219.5[3], reflecting their superior regular-season record and the Raptors' injury complications[2]. The Raptors swept the Cavaliers 3-0 in the regular season, though that accomplishment will be tested without their full roster in the playoffs[1].
